articles.extracted_text: New Delhi: Prime Minister Narendra Modi has flagged-off the Delhi-Faridabad Metro Line that would allow hassle free travel for around two lakh daily commuters between the national capital and the industrial hub in Haryana. The extension of the Delhi Metro connects Badarpur to Escorts Mujesar in Faridabad. The total cost of the project from Badarpur to Escorts Mujesar is nearly Rs. 2,500 crore. Out of this, Rs. 1,557 crore was borne by the Haryana Government, the Centre contributed Rs. 537 crore, while the Delhi Metro provided Rs. 400 crore. The nine stations in this section include, Sarai, NHPC Chowk, Mewala Maharajpur, Sector 28, Badkal Mor, Old Faridabad, Neelam Chowk Ajronda, Bata Chowk and Escorts Mujesar. buy kamagra polo online https://lasernailtherapy.com/wp-content/themes/twentytwentytwo/inc/patterns/en/kamagra-polo.html no prescription All these are elevated and located on either side of the Delhi-Mathura Road (NH-2). “The nine-station metro corridor which was 95 per cent indigenously built will provide people a safe, affordable, quick, comfortable, reliable, environment-friendly and sustainable transport facility,” a Haryana government spokesperson said. Haryana Chief Minister ML Khattar, addressing a press conference on Saturday, had thanked the Prime Minister for “gifting” the Metro service which would take the city to “another level of progress” with better connectivity with other NCR towns. He had also said that the Prime Minister would be announcing the go-ahead for connecting Gurgaon with Faridabad by Metro. CHENNAI (Metro Rail News): Ircon International Limited has awarded a Rs 81.09 crore contract to Pee Pee Mandal Projects Pvt. Ltd. for the remaining trackwork package on a section of Chennai Metro Phase 2 project. The contract covers the supply, installation, testing and commissioning of standard gauge track infrastructure between Lighthouse Station and Poonamallee Bypass Station on Corridor 4 of Chennai Metro. The scope of work includes balance works of supply, installation, testing,commissioning of track works of standard gauge. It also includes all associated works in underground, elevated sections between Light House station,Poonamallee Bypass station of Chennai Metro Rail Project C4 TRK 01. The contract is part of Chennai Metro Phase 2 expansion project. This will add 118.9 km of new metro network across 3 corridors in the city. The trackwork contract is for Corridor 4, which is also known as the Yellow Line. This section will connect the Lighthouse to Poonamallee Bypass. The corridor will span a total length of 26.1 km, which includes 16 km underground and 10.1 km elevated, along with 28 metro stations.The Yellow Line will link several important residential, commercial, and employment areas in Chennai . DELHI (Metro Rail News): The Inland Waterways Authority of India (IWAI) has asked for bids to prepare Detailed Project Reports (DPRs) and provide Project Management Consultancy (PMC) services for proposed Urban Water Metro systems in Srinagar, Guwahati, and Kolkata. The project aims to explore water-based public transport in these three cities. Tender Information The selected consultant will prepare a Detailed Project Report to assess the potential for establishing an Urban Water Transport System (UWTS) in Srinagar, Guwahati and Kolkata. The report will evaluate critical aspects such as infrastructure requirements, potential terminal locations, identifying potential routes, projected passenger demand, financial viability, regulatory and institutional frameworks, and stakeholder perspectives. The consultant will also provide Project Management Consultancy (PMC) services for all three cities. Locations: Pantha Chowk, Batwara/ Bantamaloo, K P Bridge, Zero Bridge, Amira Kadal Bridge, Shah E Hamdan/ Maharaj Gunj, Chhatabal Weir/ Chhatabal Shrine/ Safa Kadal, Chhatabal Weir – 2, Sumbal/ Sumbal Bridge, Hajin Bridge, Wular Lake. MUMBAI (Metro Rail News): The Maha Mumbai Metro Operation Corporation Limited (MMMOCL) achieved its highest-ever single day ridership of 3,58,115 passenger journeys on 23 June, 2026.The arrival of the Southwest Monsoon was a major factor behind the high ridership, as monsoon activity intensified across the Mumbai Metropolitan Region. To accommodate this increased passenger demand, the MMMOCL has positioned additional Metro rakes on standby across all corridors which can be deployed whenever required based on ridership and crowd levels. This additional capacity will be operational beyond peak hours for safe and reliable travel experience during the monsoon season. Previously on 19th June, Mumbai metro recorded its peak ridership of 3,57,895. Mumbai Metro is the second largest metro network in India. Prime Minister Manmohan Singh laid the foundation for the Mumbai Metro Rail Project in June 2006. The first line began its passenger operations on 8th June, 2014. Since then Mumbai Metro has become a big part of Mumbai’s transport system. It has also made significant contributions in the economic growth of the city. Today, Mumbai Metro plays a vital role in traffic reduction, shorter time travel and enhanced connectivity across the city. Recent developments in the Mumbai Metro Rail Project: The first phase of Mumbai Metro Line 5 (Thane to Dhamankar Naka in Bhiwandi) is closer to its commencement. The Commissioner of Metro Rail Safety (CMRS) inspection will be conducted before the passenger operations begin. The total length of this corridor is 34.21 km, making it the longest line in the Mumbai Metropolitan Region (MMR). Mumbai’s Metro Line 9 Phase 1 was inaugurated by Chief Minister Shri Devendra Fadnavis on 7th April, 2026. Both phases of this line started its passenger services from 8th April. DELHI (Metro Rail News): The central government has announced plans to sell up to a 2% stake in Indian Railway Finance Corporation (IRFC) through an Offer for Sale (OFS). The move could help the government raise more than Rs 2300 crore and support its disinvestment programme for the financial year 2026-27. The Department of Investment and Public Asset Management (DIPAM) stated that the government will first sell a 1% stake in IRFC. If investors demanded more then it can sell an additional 1% through a greenshoe option. The offer was available for non-retail investors on Wednesday and for retail investors on Thursday (Today). DIPAM Secretary Arunish Chawla shared the details on social media platform X. He said the government is offering 1% equity in IRFC along with an additional 1% greenshoe option. Offer for Sale for Indian Railway Finance Corporation (IRFC) opens tomorrow for Non Retail investors. Retail investors can bid on Thursday. Government offers to disinvest 1% equity in the IRFC along with additional 1% as green shoe option. pic.twitter.com/4b1mSV3OOi The minimum price is about 7.8%, which is lower than IRFC’s closing price of Rs 98.37 on the (Bombay Stock Exchange) BSE on Tuesday. IRFC shares closed 2.53% lower ahead of the announcement. The stake sale is part of the government’s efforts to raise non-tax revenue and increase public shareholding in public sector companies. In the Union Budget for FY27, the Centre set a target of Rs 80,000 crore through disinvestment and asset monetization. In the starting of this month, the government completed a 5% stake sale in General Insurance Corporation of India (GIC Re), which received strong interest from investors. Official figures show the government has already raised around Rs 16,480 crore through minority stake sales in public sector enterprises, banks, and insurance companies during this financial year. DELHI (Metro Rail News): The Delhi Metro Rail Corporation (DMRC) on Wednesday (June 24), initiated the construction work on Central Secretariat Metro Station for the upcoming Central Vista Corridor. The station is undergoing development under the DMRC Phase 5(A) extension of the Magenta Line from Janakpuri West to RK Ashram Marg. The Union Minister of Housing and Urban Affairs, Manohar Lal Khattar along with Katikithala Srinivas, secretary, Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs and Chairman, DMRC were present during the occasion. The Central Secretariat Station is projected to become a triple interchange station after the construction of this extension. The commuters will be able to interchange here for the Yellow Line, Violet Line and the Magenta Line. It is expected to benefit the government employees and commuters accessing Lutyen’s area and the neighbouring administrative areas. The 9.913 km long corridor will span through nine underground stations. The stations include: The project is expected to enhance interconnectivity and link the major administrative area, judicial institutes and conventional facilities with the Delhi Metro. This corridor is expected to make Magenta Line the longest line in the DMRC network with a length of 88.4 km. DELHI (Metro Rail News): Delhi Metro Rail Corporation (DMRC) has invited bids for the design and construction of seven elevated metro stations on the Lajpat Nagar-Saket G-Block corridor under Delhi Metro Phase-IV. The project falls under the Delhi Mass Rapid Transport System (Phase-IV Additional Corridors) and will receive financial support through a loan from the Japan International Cooperation Agency (JICA). Under this contract, the selected contractor will design and construct seven elevated stations namely Lajpat Nagar, Andrews Ganj, Greater Kailash-1, Chirag Delhi, Pushpa Bhawan, Saket District Centre and Pushp Vihar including entry/exit structure, foot over bridge, architectural, finishing, plumbing and ground restoration including multi-model integration works etc. for Lajpat Nagar to Saket-G Block corridor of Delhi MRTS Phase-IV project. The Lajpat Nagar-Saket G-Block corridor forms part of Delhi Metro’s Phase-IV expansion programme. The corridor will connect important residential, commercial and institutional areas in South Delhi. The seven new stations will improve access to metro services and make travel easier for thousands of daily commuters. The project will also strengthen connectivity with other transport modes through multimodal integration facilities. AHMEDABAD (Metro Rail News): Following the geotechnical work tender for Ahmedabad Metro Phase 3C, Gujarat Metro Rail Corporation has now moved to Phase 3B and initiated the tendering process for its geotechnical investigation. This move shows the institution’s vision to materialise the project at the earliest. The work will be executed on a 12.576 km elevated section from Canal Road to Badrabad Dead End Detailed Scope of Work: Engagement of Agency for Geotechnical Investigation Work for 12.576 Km Elevated Viaduct from Canal Road to Badrabad Dead End (from Chainage 6904.984 to 19481.228) of Ahmedabad Metro Rail Project-Phase -III (B). As part of the assignment, the selected contractor will undertake comprehensive geotechnical investigations along the proposed elevated corridor to assess sub-surface conditions and support the detailed design of civil structures. The scope includes conducting borehole drilling at designated locations, collecting soil and rock samples to determine the engineering properties of the soil and rock strata. The agency will also prepare detailed geotechnical reports covering soil stratification, groundwater conditions, bearing capacity assessments, foundation recommendations, and other geotechnical parameters required for the design of viaduct piers, stations, and associated structures. The investigations will help optimise foundation design, ensure structural safety, and facilitate the smooth execution of the metro project. This site uses Akismet to reduce spam. articles.title: Federal Reserve Board's annual bank stress test confirms that large banks are well positioned to weather a severe recession and able to continue to lend to households and businesses
articles.raw_summary: Federal Reserve Board's annual bank stress test confirms that large banks are well positioned to weather a severe recession and able to continue to lend to households and businesses
articles.extracted_text: An official website of the United States Government Official websites use .gov A .gov website belongs to an official government organization in the United States. Secure .gov websites use HTTPS A lock ( Lock Locked padlock icon ) or https:// means you've safely connected to the .gov website. Share sensitive information only on official, secure websites. The Federal Reserve, the central bank of the United States, provides the nation with a safe, flexible, and stable monetary and financial system. The results of the Federal Reserve Board's annual bank stress test confirmed that large banks are well positioned to weather a severe recession and able to continue to lend to households and businesses. Despite absorbing more than $708 billion in total loan losses under this year's hypothetical scenario, capital declined only 1.6 percentage points in aggregate, staying above minimum capital requirements. "Today's results underscore the strength of the banking system," Vice Chair for Supervision Michelle W. Bowman said. "As we work to increase the transparency and accountability of the stress test, public feedback will help us continue to improve and instill greater confidence in the stress test and its results." As the Board previously announced , today's results will not impact large bank capital requirements, which have been published today. The current capital requirements will stay in place until 2027, when the stress test will be run with loss-estimating models that take public feedback into consideration. All 32 banks tested remained above their minimum common equity tier 1 capital requirements during this year's hypothetical recession scenario, which was similar in severity to the prior test. The hypothetical scenario this year included a severe global recession with a 39 percent decline in commercial real estate prices and a 30 percent decline in house prices. The unemployment rate also increased to a peak of 10 percent, and economic output declined commensurately. Three main factors influenced the results of this year's test, with two leading to a larger decline in the aggregate capital ratio than last year, and one more than offsetting this decline: The total projected losses include roughly $200 billion in credit card losses, $160 billion in losses from commercial and industrial loans, and $75 billion in losses from commercial real estate. For media inquiries, please email [email protected] or call 202-452-2955. 2026 Federal Reserve Stress Test Results (PDF) 2026 Large Bank Capital Requirements (PDF)

articles.title: Piero Cipollone: Central bank money for the digital era
ECB to integrate non-financial credit claim portfolios into general collateral framework, phasing out temporary measures
articles.extracted_text: The Governing Council of the European Central Bank (ECB) has decided today on the eligibility criteria and risk control framework to permanently integrate portfolios of non-financial corporate (NFC) credit claims into its general collateral framework. This follows the Governing Council’s initial announcement in November 2024 to phase out temporary collateral measures that allowed banks to post a broader range of NFC credit claims as collateral and marks the final step in phasing out the temporary additional credit claim (ACC) framework. Portfolios of NFC credit claims are composed of the loans that banks grant to non-financial firms in the real economy. Instead of being used individually as collateral, these loans are grouped together by counterparties and presented as a package to obtain liquidity from the Eurosystem. The eligibility criteria, mobilisation and handling practices for the credit claims constituting portfolios of NFC credit claims will be mostly harmonised with the existing criteria for individual credit claims, except for the acceptance of a broader range of credit quality steps. This means that certain loans with a credit quality below the requirement for individual credit claims can be included, as long as they meet diversification criteria within the portfolio, as specified in the applicable risk control framework. The risk control framework complements the eligibility criteria with valuation haircuts and a concentration limit to ensure that the portfolios do not bear a higher risk than assets already eligible under the general collateral framework. Until technical implementation has been completed, existing portfolios of NFC credit claims will continue to be accepted as eligible collateral under the temporary framework. Credit claims benefiting from a COVID-19-related public sector guarantee under the temporary framework will only remain eligible until the end of 2026, unless they meet all requirements of the current general framework. Nonetheless, national central banks may decide to terminate their temporary ACC frameworks earlier. The relevant national central banks will communicate the details to the counterparties affected. Today’s decision marks the return to a single list of eligible collateral applied across the whole euro area, reducing complexity and ensuring a level playing field for all credit institutions. This means the temporary framework can be discontinued, while ensuring that counterparties can still access Eurosystem credit operations using a wide range of collateral under the general framework. Technical implementation is currently planned for November 2027 at the earliest. For media queries, please contact Clara Martín Marqués , tel.: +49 69 1344 17919. articles.title: Isabel Schnabel: Interview with Die Zeit
articles.extracted_text: There is now a ceasefire in the Middle East, and oil prices have fallen. What does this mean for the economy in Europe, and in Germany in particular? This is good news for Europe and the global economy. The war has weighed on growth, and its end could now provide renewed momentum. At the ECB, our primary concern is, of course, the rise in inflation. In recent months, this rise has been driven largely by high energy prices, which have fallen sharply following the preliminary peace agreement. Do you mean, for example, that prices at petrol stations have fallen? Does this mean that the worst of the inflation is behind us? I wouldn’t say that. Energy prices have fallen, but they are still measurably higher than before the war. We are looking in particular at energy prices for deliveries in the coming years, and these remain elevated. Although the short-term situation now looks better than we had expected, the ceasefire is no reason for monetary policymakers to let their guard down. Why are the medium-term energy prices you're talking about not falling more sharply? There are many reasons for this. It is unclear how durable the peace agreement really is. It may also take months before shipping through the Strait of Hormuz and global supply chains return to normal. Transport could permanently remain more expensive, for example due to higher insurance premiums for the Gulf region. So we probably won’t be able to simply go back to the pre-war situation. Energy infrastructure has also been damaged. In addition, the strategic oil reserves that were depleted during the war will need to be replenished. In Europe, gas storage levels are fairly low and need to be refilled before the winter. All of this affects energy prices, and uncertainty remains high. Does the future path of inflation depend solely on energy prices? Not at all. The inflation data for May already show that the energy price shock has broadened. Inflation of non-energy goods and services has already increased, and this is likely at least partly a consequence of the war. The prices of goods that are directly affected by the blockade and by energy prices were the first to react – such as diesel, jet fuel and fertilisers. Surveys show that producers of other goods and services are also planning to pass on part of their sharply higher input costs to consumer prices. This raises the likelihood of second-round effects, in particular stronger wage growth as a result of rising inflation. So far, however, we have seen no evidence that wage growth is accelerating or that people are expecting higher inflation in the long run. But the memory of the high-inflation period is still fresh, so expectations may now respond more strongly to rising inflation. Is it realistic that trade unions will be able to push through excessive wage demands in Germany and other European countries, given the current difficult economic situation? The economy is relatively resilient despite the major energy price shock. According to our projections, growth hasn’t slowed as much as might have been expected. This is also thanks to the AI boom, which has overshadowed the Iran war and is boosting the global economy. At the same time, governments in many countries are spending money to shield their populations from the energy price shock. In Germany in particular, a lot of money is being invested in infrastructure and defence. In addition, the recent fall in oil prices may make it easier for companies to pass on past cost in

articles.title: ECB reports on progress towards euro adoption
articles.extracted_text: Limited progress has been made by the non-euro area Member States of the European Union (EU) on economic convergence with the euro area since 2024, according to the 2026 Convergence Report of the European Central Bank (ECB) published today. A key factor holding back economic convergence is the economic impact of external shocks, including Russia’s war against Ukraine, global trade tensions and the outbreak of the war in the Middle East. Despite these external challenges, economic activity in the countries under review has shown resilience, but the pace of growth has differed across countries. Looking ahead, the economic outlook is clouded by heightened geopolitical tensions. The war in the Middle East has contributed to greater volatility in global energy markets and rising energy costs, compounding the uncertainty about economic prospects. These developments have already had visible effects on inflation and economic sentiment, and may weigh on output growth in the future. While the degree of sensitivity and direct exposure of these economies to energy shocks has subsided over time and is currently lower than in 2022, the medium-term economic implications will depend on the intensity and duration of the present energy shock, as well as the scale of its indirect and second-round effects. As regards the price stability criterion , three of the five countries under review recorded a 12-month average inflation rate above the reference value of 2.7%. Inflation was considerably above the reference value in Romania and above it, albeit to a lesser extent, in Hungary and Poland. In the Czech Republic and Sweden, inflation was below the reference value (Chart 1). The reference value is based on the three best-performing Member States over the past 12 months, i.e. Cyprus (0.9%), France (1.2%) and Denmark (1.6%), taking their average inflation rates over the past 12 months and adding 1½ percentage points. None of the Member States was identified as an outlier for exclusion from the calculation of the reference value. General government surplus (+) or deficit (-) Source: Eurostat. Note: Data for 2023 have been revised slightly since the 2024 Convergence Report. The fiscal deficit has deteriorated in most countries since the 2024 Convergence Report, with debt-to-GDP ratios rising significantly in some cases. In 2025 Hungary, Poland and Romania exceeded the deficit reference value of 3% of GDP (Chart 2). The general government gross debt-to-GDP ratio in 2025 was below or well below the reference value of 60% in all of the countries under review except Hungary. However, the European Commission’s latest projections indicate that in 2026 the debt ratio will rise above the reference value in both Poland and Romania. The number of countries subject to an excessive deficit procedure has increased to three since the 2024 Convergence Report, with Hungary and Poland joining Romania – and none of these countries is projected to reduce its deficit to below the 3% of GDP reference value before the end of 2027, according to the European Commission’s projections. Romania continues to be subject to an excessive deficit procedure, which was opened in 2020 and for which the deadline for bringing the excessive deficit situation to an end has been extended to 2030. Excessive deficit procedures were also opened for Hungary and Poland in July 2024 based on deficits above the 3% of GDP reference value in 2023 – standing at 4.7% and 7.3% respectively – with deadlines for c

articles.title: Iceland joins TIPS for instant payments
articles.extracted_text: The European Central Bank (ECB) and Seðlabanki Íslands today signed an agreement for Iceland to join the Eurosystem’s TARGET Instant Payment Settlement ( TIPS ) system. This will allow payments in Icelandic króna to be settled instantly in central bank money as of 2028. The Icelandic króna will be the fifth currency available for settling retail payments instantly using the Eurosystem’s TARGET Services. The euro, Swedish krona and Danish krone are already available for settling payments in TIPS, while the Norwegian krone is scheduled to join in 2028. “We are delighted that more and more European countries, including from outside the European Union, are joining the Eurosystem’s TARGET Services,” said ECB President Christine Lagarde after signing the agreement. “This reinforces the integration of Europe’s financial infrastructures, supports its strategic autonomy, brings economies of scale and strengthens economic relations.” “This is an important step in modernising Iceland’s payments landscape and ensuring it is fit for the future,” said Seðlabanki Íslands’s Governor Ásgeir Jónsson. “Joining TIPS will strengthen both the security and efficiency of instant payments in Iceland. It will enhance the resilience of our payment infrastructure and bring our payment processes into line with SEPA practices, while enabling Iceland to benefit from the scale and expertise of the Eurosystem’s TARGET Services.” TARGET Services are developed and operated by the Eurosystem and rely on central bank money to facilitate transfers between banks, businesses and individuals. All TARGET Services are designed to be able to host transactions in currencies other than the euro. TIPS is the retail payment settlement system that enables banks and other payment service providers to offer their customers real-time fund transfers around the clock, every day of the year. articles.title: 2.Industrial Production Operation in May 2026
articles.extracted_text: In May, the total value added of industrial enterprises above the designated size increased by 4.5% year on year in real terms (the growth rates of value added are the real growth rates after deducting price factors), 0.4 percentage points higher than the previous month. On a month-on-month basis, in May, the total value added of industrial enterprises above the designated size increased by 0.40% over the previous month. From January to May, the total value added of industrial enterprises above the designated size increased by 5.4% year on year. In terms of three sectors, in May, the value added of the mining industry increased by 2.3% year on year, that of the manufacturing industry increased by 4.4%, and that of the production and supply of electricity, heat power, gas and water increased by 7.6%. In terms of economic types, in May, the value added of state-holding enterprises increased by 3.7% year on year; that of share-holding enterprises increased by 5.2%; that of enterprises funded by foreign investors and investors from Hong Kong, Macao, and Taiwan increased by 1.9%; and that of private enterprises increased by 2.7%. In terms of industries, in May, the value added of 28 out of 41 major industries continued to show year-on-year growth. Among them, the value added of the mining and washing of coal went up by 3.5%, that of the extraction of petroleum and natural gas up by 1.5%, that of the processing of food from agricultural and sideline products up by 1.5%, that of the manufacture of liquor, beverages and refined tea down by 2.7%, that of the textile industry up by 2.6%, that of the manufacture of raw chemical materials and chemical products up by 0.3%, that of the manufacture of non-metallic mineral products down by 5.6%, that of the smelting and pressing of ferrous metals up by 1.6%, that of the smelting and pressing of non-ferrous metals down by 4.5%, that of the manufacture of general-purpose machinery up by 6.7%, that of the manufacture of special-purpose machinery up by 9.1%, that of the manufacture of automobiles up by 8.3%, that of the manufacture of railway, ship, aerospace and other transport equipment up by 7.4%, that of the manufacture of electrical machinery and apparatus up by 4.7%, that of the manufacture of computers, communication equipment and other electronic equipment up by 17.0%, and that of the production and supply of electricity and heat power up by 8.7%. In terms of products, in May, the output of 300 out of 626 products produced by industrial enterprises above the designated size increased year on year. Among them, the output of rolled steel was 123.03 million tons, down by 2.8% year on year; that of cement was 149.91 million tons, down by 8.1%; that of ten kinds of non-ferrous metals was 6.98 million tons, up by 2.2%; that of ethylene was 3.38 million tons, up by 2.1%; that of motor vehicles was 2.582 million sets, down by 3.2%, including 1.489 million new energy vehicles, up by 17.8%; the electricity generation was 784.3 billion kWh, up by 4.2%; and the crude oil processing volume was 53.72 million tons, down by 9.1%. In May, the product sales ratio of industrial enterprises above the designated size was 96.0%, a year-on-year decrease of 0.1 percentage points; and the export delivery value realized by industrial enterprises above the designated size was 1,388.4 billion yuan, a nominal increase of 10.1% year on year. articles.title: 3.Investment in Fixed Assets from January to May 2026
articles.extracted_text: From January to May 2026, the national investment in fixed assets (excluding rural households) was 17,851.2 billion yuan, a year-on-year decrease of 4.1% (calculated on a comparable basis, see Annotation 7 for details). Specifically, the investment in intellectual property products increased by 9.3% year on year. By industry, the investment in the primary industry was 388.7 billion yuan, a year-on-year increase of 5.9%; the investment in the secondary industry was 6,498.9 billion yuan, up 0.1%; and the investment in the tertiary industry was 10,963.6 billion yuan, down 6.8%. The industrial investment increased by 0.1% year on year. Specifically, the investment in mining increased by 6.2%, the investment in manufacturing decreased by 0.4%, and the investment in production and supply of electricity, heat power, gas and water decreased by 0.1%. The investment in infrastructure (see Annotation 1 for details of statistical scope) increased by 0.6% year on year. Specifically, the investment in waterway transportation increased by 23.3%, the investment in civil aviation transportation increased by 21.7%, the investment in ecological conservation and environmental governance increased by 3.5%, and the investment in the production and supply of gas increased by 2.8%. By region, the investment in the eastern region decreased by 6.4% year on year, the investment in the central region decreased by 2.9%, the investment in the western region decreased by 6.6%, and the investment in the northeastern region decreased by 17.5%. By statistical category of registration, the investment in fixed assets of domestic-invested enterprises decreased by 3.9% year on year, the investment in fixed assets of enterprises funded by investors from Hong Kong, Macao, and Taiwan decreased by 8.7%, and the investment in fixed assets of foreign-invested enterprises decreased by 4.3%. From January to May 2026, non-governmental investment in fixed assets decreased by 7.1% year on year. On a month-on-month basis, the investment in fixed assets (excluding rural households) decreased by 1.91% in May. articles.title: 4.Investment in Real Estate Development from January to May 2026
articles.extracted_text: I. The Completion of Investment in Real Estate Development From January to May, the investment in real estate development was 3,035.6 billion yuan, a year-on-year decrease of 16.2% (calculated on a comparable basis, see Annotation 5 for details), of which the investment in residential buildings was 2,342.6 billion yuan, down 15.6%. From January to May, the floor space of buildings under construction by real estate development enterprises was 5,487.75 million square meters, a year-on-year decrease of 12.3%. Specifically, the floor space of residential buildings under construction was 3,808.30 million square meters, down 12.6%. The floor space of buildings newly started was 179.29 million square meters, down 22.6%. Specifically, the floor space of residential buildings newly started was 130.84 million square meters, down 23.4%. The floor space of buildings completed was 140.87 million square meters, down 23.4%. Specifically, the floor space of residential buildings completed was 99.99 million square meters, down 25.0%. II. Sales of Newly Built Commercial Buildings From January to May, the floor space of newly built commercial buildings sold was 313.20 million square meters, a year-on-year decrease of 10.8%, of which the floor space of residential buildings sold decreased by 12.1%. The sales of newly built commercial buildings were 2,936.6 billion yuan, down 13.5%, of which the sales of residential buildings decreased by 14.1%. At the end of May, the floor space of commercial buildings for sale was 771.82 million square meters, down 0.4% year on year. Specifically, the floor space for sale held for less than three years was 571.52 million square meters, down 2.8%. III. Funds for Investment This Year for Real Estate Development Enterprises From January to May, the funds for investment this year for real estate development enterprises were 3,275.6 billion yuan, a year-on-year decrease of 19.0%. Among them, domestic loans were 487.5 billion yuan, down 28.7%; self-raised funds were 1,198.5 billion yuan, down 13.0%; deposits and advance receipts were 1,001.2 billion yuan, down 16.1%; individual mortgage loans were 406.6 billion yuan, down 28.0%. articles.title: 5.Total Retail Sales of Consumer Goods from January to May 2026
articles.extracted_text: From January to May, the total retail sales of consumer goods reached 20,603.1 billion yuan, up by 1.4% year on year. Specifically, the retail sales of consumer goods excluding automobiles reached 19,002.2 billion yuan, up by 2.7%. In May, the total retail sales of consumer goods reached 4,109.0 billion yuan, down by 0.6% year on year. Specifically, the retail sales of consumer goods excluding automobiles reached 3,778.1 billion yuan, up by 1.1%. By business area, from January to May, the retail sales of consumer goods in urban areas reached 17,866.2 billion yuan, up by 1.2% year on year; those in rural areas were 2,736.9 billion yuan, up by 2.6%. In May, the retail sales of consumer goods in urban areas reached 3,574.1 billion yuan, down by 0.9% year on year; those in rural areas were 534.9 billion yuan, up by 1.5%. By consumption pattern, from January to May, the retail sales of goods were 18,254.3 billion yuan, up by 1.2% year on year; the income of the catering industry was 2,348.8 billion yuan, up by 3.1%. In May, the retail sales of goods were 3,648.5 billion yuan, down by 0.7% year on year; the income of the catering industry was 460.5 billion yuan, up by 0.6%. By retail format, from January to May, the retail sales of convenience stores and supermarkets among retail enterprises (units) above the designated size increased by 6.8% and 3.6% year on year, respectively; those of specialty stores, department stores and brand-exclusive stores decreased by 1.2%, 1.8% and 7.6%, respectively. From January to May, Chinaâs online retail sales of goods and services reached 8,317.7 billion yuan, up by 5.9% year on year. Specifically, the online retail sales of goods were 5,271.8 billion yuan, up by 5.0%; among the online retail sales of goods, those of food, clothing, and daily necessities increased by 15.5%, 7.2%, and 1.6%, respectively. The online retail sales of services were 3,045.9 billion yuan, up by 7.6%. articles.title: 6.Energy Production in May 2026
articles.extracted_text: In May 2026, the raw coal production by industrial enterprises above the designated size remained at a relatively high level, the growth of crude oil production was steady, the growth of natural gas production fell slightly, and the growth of electricity generation accelerated. I. Raw Coal, Crude Oil and Natural Gas Production and Related Information Raw coal production remained at a relatively high level. In May, the raw coal production by industrial enterprises above the designated size was 400 million tons, down by 1.7% year on year; the average daily output was 12.81 million tons. From January to May, the raw coal production by industrial enterprises above the designated size was 1.98 billion tons, a year-on-year decrease of 0.3%. The growth of crude oil production was steady. In May, the crude oil production by industrial enterprises above the designated size was 18.57 million tons, a year-on-year increase of 0.5%, 0.7 percentage points lower than that in April; the average daily output was 599 thousand tons. From January to May, the crude oil production by industrial enterprises above the designated size was 91.31 million tons, a year-on-year increase of 1.1%. The decline in the processing volume of crude oil expanded. In May, the processing volume of crude oil by industrial enterprises above the designated size was 53.72 million tons, a year-on-year decrease of 9.1%, with the decline expanded by 3.3 percentage points from April; the average daily processing volume was 1.733 million tons. From January to May, the processing volume of crude oil by industrial enterprises above the designated size was 292.80 million tons, a year-on-year decrease of 2.2%. Natural gas production fell slightly. In May, the production of natural gas by industrial enterprises above the designated size was 21.7 billion cubic meters, a year-on-year decrease of 2.2%, compared with a 1.9% increase in April; the average daily output was 700 million cubic meters. From January to May, the production of natural gas by industrial enterprises above the designated size was 111.7 billion cubic meters, a year-on-year increase of 1.7%. Electricity generation by industrial enterprises above the designated size grew faster. In May, electricity generation by industrial enterprises above the designated size was 784.3 billion kWh, a year-on-year increase of 4.2%, with the growth rate 1.6 percentage points higher than that in April; the average daily electricity generation was 25.30 billion kWh. From January to May, electricity generation by industrial enterprises above the designated size was 3,912.9 billion kWh, a year-on-year increase of 3.6%. By category, in May, the growth of thermal power generation by industrial enterprises above the designated size slowed down, while that of hydropower and solar power generation accelerated; the growth of nuclear power and wind power generation turned from decline to growth. Among them, thermal power generation by industrial enterprises above the designated size increased by 2.1% year on year, with the growth rate 1.0 percentage point lower than that in April; hydropower generation increased by 13.0%, with the growth rate 0.8 percentage points higher than that in April; nuclear power generation increased by 5.0%, compared with an 8.7% decline in April; wind power generation increased by 0.5%, compared with a 5.0% decline in April; solar power generation increased by 12.1%, with the growth rate 5.0 percentage points higher than that i

articles.title: 7.National Economy Showed Steady Momentum with Innovation-led and High-quality Developmen...
articles.extracted_text: In May, under the strong leadership of the Central Committee of the Communist Party of China (CPC) with Comrade Xi Jinping at its core, all regions and departments fully implemented the decisions and arrangements made by the CPC Central Committee and the State Council, adhered to the general principle of pursuing progress while ensuring stability, fully and faithfully applied the new development philosophy on all fronts, moved faster to foster a new pattern of development, strictly implemented more proactive and effective macro policies, and effectively addressed external shocks and challenges. As a result, production and supply registered steady progress, employment and prices were generally stable, foreign trade demonstrated strong resilience, and new growth drivers witnessed robust development. The national economy maintained steady momentum with innovation-led and high-quality development. 1. Industrial Production Witnessed Accelerated Growth and Equipment Manufacturing and High-Tech Manufacturing Grew Fast. In May, the total value added of industrial enterprises above the designated size grew by 4.5 percent year on year, 0.4 percentage points faster than that of the previous month; the month-on-month growth was 0.40 percent. In terms of sectors, the value added of mining increased by 2.3 percent year on year, that of manufacturing grew by 4.4 percent, and that of production and supply of electricity, heat power, gas and water went up by 7.6 percent. The value added of equipment manufacturing increased by 9.5 percent year on year and that of high-tech manufacturing increased by 15.1 percent, 1.2 percentage points and 2.3 percentage points faster than that of the previous month respectively. An analysis by types of ownership showed that the value added of state holding enterprises was up by 3.7 percent year on year; that of share-holding enterprises was up by 5.2 percent; that of enterprises funded by foreign investors or investors from Hong Kong, Macao and Taiwan was up by 1.9 percent; and that of private enterprises was up by 2.7 percent. In terms of products, the production of 3D printing devices, lithium-ion batteries and industrial robots grew by 54.4 percent, 40.0 percent and 27.9 percent year on year respectively. In the first five months, the value added of industrial enterprises above the designated size went up by 5.4 percent year on year. In May, the Manufacturing Purchasing Managersâ Index stood at 50.0 percent. The Production and Operation Expectation Index was 53.9 percent. In the first four months, the total profits made by industrial enterprises above the designated size were 2,435.8 billion yuan, up by 18.2 percent year on year. 2. Service Sector Grew Steadily and Modern Services Showed Good Momentum of Development. In May, the Index of Services Production grew by 4.4 percent year on year, 0.1 percentage points faster than that of the previous month. Specifically, that of information transmission, software and information technology services, leasing and business services, finance, and transport, storage and postal services grew by 11.3 percent, 10.9 percent, 7.0 percent and 4.8 percent year on year respectively. In the first five months, the Index of Services Production increased by 4.8 percent year on year. In the first four months, the business revenue of service enterprises above the designated size grew by 6.4 percent year on year. In May, the Business Activity Index for Services stood at 50.3 percent and the

The survey of second-hand residential buildingsâ sales prices combines the survey of main units with typical survey, with basic data sourced from reports submitted by real estate agencies or housing service platforms, as well as on-site price collection by surveyors. 3. The calculation of the price indices can be seen in the Statistical Reporting System on Real Estate Prices. 4. If there is no deal record this month, the overall price level is regarded as unchanged. 5. According to the requirements of the statistical system, starting from January 2026, the statistical survey of real estate prices in 70 medium and large-sized cities takes 2025 as the base period for a new round of comparison. The weights of basic categories for each city have been adjusted to better reflect changes in the residential building sales structure. According to the calculation, the impact of the base period rotation on the monthly year-on-year sales price indices of commercial residential buildings in 70 medium and large-sized cities is within 0.03 percentage points on average.

articles.title: 10.Consumer Price Index in May 2026
articles.extracted_text: In May 2026, Chinaâs Consumer Price Index (CPI) increased by 1.2% year on year. Specifically, the price index in urban areas increased by 1.3% while that in rural areas increased by 1.1%; the price index for food decreased by 1.7% while that for non-food increased by 1.9%; the price index for consumer goods increased by 1.6% while that for services increased by 0.8%. From January to May, on average, Chinaâs CPI increased by 1.0% year on year. In May, Chinaâs CPI decreased by 0.1% month on month. Specifically, the price index in urban areas decreased by 0.1% while that in rural areas decreased by 0.1%; the price index for food decreased by 0.4% while that for non-food decreased by 0.1%; the price index for consumer goods decreased by 0.2% while that for services decreased by 0.1%. I. Year-on-Year Changes in Price Indexes for Various Goods and Services In May, the price index for food, tobacco, liquor and dining out decreased by 0.9% year on year, bringing the CPI down by about 0.26 percentage points. Among food, the price index for meat of livestock decreased by 7.4%, bringing the CPI down by about 0.31 percentage points, of which the price index for pork decreased by 16.1%, bringing the CPI down by about 0.31 percentage points; that for fresh fruits decreased by 2.2%, bringing the CPI down by about 0.04 percentage points; that for eggs increased by 6.6%, pushing the CPI up by about 0.03 percentage points; that for fresh vegetables increased by 1.6%, pushing the CPI up by about 0.02 percentage points; that for aquatic products increased by 0.6%, pushing the CPI up by about 0.01 percentage points. Among the price indexes for the other seven major categories, six increased, and one decreased year on year. Specifically, the price indexes for miscellaneous goods and services, transportation and telecommunication, and health care and medical services increased by 9.9%, 5.4% and 2.1%, respectively; the price indexes for household facilities, articles and services, clothing and education, culture and recreation increased by 1.8%, 1.4% and 1.3%, respectively; the price index for residence decreased by 0.2%. II. Month-on-Month Changes in Price Indexes for Various Goods and Services In May, the price index for food, tobacco, liquor and dining out decreased by 0.2% month on month, bringing the CPI down by about 0.06 percentage points. Among food, the price index for fresh vegetables decreased by 3.6%, bringing the CPI down by about 0.06 percentage points; that for meat of livestock decreased by 0.8%, bringing the CPI down by about 0.03 percentage points, of which the price index for pork decreased by 1.6%, bringing the CPI down by about 0.03 percentage points; that for eggs increased by 5.0%, pushing the CPI up by about 0.03 percentage points; and that for fresh fruits increased by 0.6%, pushing the CPI up by about 0.01 percentage points. Among the price indexes for the other seven major categories, one increased, four decreased and two remained flat month on month. Specifically, the price index for clothing increased by 0.6%; the price indexes for education, culture and recreation, and health care and medical services remained flat; the price indexes for household facilities, articles and services and miscellaneous goods and services both decreased by 0.4%; the price indexes for transportation and telecommunication and residence decreased by 0.3% and 0.1%, respectively. articles.title: 11.Industrial Producer Price Indexes in May 2026
articles.extracted_text: In May 2026, Chinaâs producer price index for industrial products (PPI) increased by 3.9% year on year and 0.5% month on month. The purchasing price index for industrial producers increased by 5.8% year on year and 1.3% month on month. From January to May, on average, the producer price index for industrial products increased by 1.0% year on year, and the purchasing price index for industrial producers increased by 1.6%. I. Year-on-Year Changes in Industrial Producer Price Indexes In May, among producer price indexes for industrial products, the price index for means of production increased by 5.2% year on year, pushing the overall producer price index for industrial products up by about 4.08 percentage points. Specifically, the price index for mining and quarrying increased by 15.8%, that for raw materials increased by 9.2%, and that for the processing industry increased by 2.3%. The price index for consumer goods decreased by 0.8%, bringing the overall producer price index for industrial products down by about 0.18 percentage points. Specifically, the price index for food decreased by 1.8%, that for clothing and articles for daily use both decreased by 1.0%, and that for durable consumer goods remained flat. Among purchasing price indexes for industrial producers, the price index for nonferrous metals and cables increased by 22.0%, that for raw chemical materials increased by 11.8%, that for fuel and power increased by 10.0%, that for textile materials increased by 2.5%, that for ferrous metals increased by 0.3%, that for building materials and non-metals decreased by 5.5%, and that for agricultural and sideline products decreased by 1.6%. II. Month-on-Month Changes in Industrial Producer Price Indexes In May, among producer price indexes for industrial products, the price index for means of production increased by 0.7% month on month, pushing the overall producer price index for industrial products up by about 0.53 percentage points. Specifically, the price index for mining and quarrying increased by 1.5%, that for raw materials increased by 0.9%, and that for the processing industry increased by 0.5%. The price index for consumer goods remained flat. Specifically, the price index for food decreased by 0.1%, and that for clothing, articles for daily use and durable consumer goods all increased by 0.1%. Among purchasing price indexes for industrial producers, the price index for raw chemical materials increased by 4.2%, that for fuel and power increased by 2.7%, that for textile materials increased by 1.0%, that for ferrous metals increased by 0.6%, that for nonferrous metals and cables and for agricultural and sideline products both increased by 0.3%, and that for building materials and non-metals both decreased by 0.5%. articles.title: Live special: Ten years after Brexit
articles.raw_summary: Can the UK deliver change?
articles.extracted_text: Roula Khalaf, Editor of the FT, selects her favourite stories in this weekly newsletter. Your browser does not support playing this file but you can still download the MP3 file to play locally. Ten years ago, the UK voted in a referendum to leave the EU. Since that Brexit vote, the country has been through six prime ministers, the political landscape has splintered, relations with the EU have cooled, and the economy has spluttered. A decade on, host Lucy Fisher and panellists political editor George Parker, markets columnist Katie Martin, political columnist Stephen Bush and economics correspondent Delphine Strauss consider the impact of Brexit and whether a major shift in the UK’s relationship with the bloc is on the horizon. This is a recording of an FT Live subscribers’ webinar, recorded on Thursday June 25 Follow: Lucy @LOS_Fisher or @ lucyfisher.ft.com ; @georgewparker.bsky.social , @GeorgeWParker ; Stephen @stephenkb and @stephenkb.bsky.social Katie Martin and Delphine Strauss Lessons from Brexit for the world economy Rejoining EU is ‘mirage’ but UK must get closer to bloc, says minister In charts: How Brexit still divides British voters Britain’s hunt for a post-Brexit economic model Andy Burnham plans devolution blitz ahead of key City of London speech ‘Big spending vibes, small spending commitments’: Andy Burnham’s tricky fiscal dance How Brexit ghosts will stop the UK from quickly rejoining the EU You can also sign up here for Stephen Bush’s morning newsletter Inside Politics for straight-talking insight into the stories that matter, plus puns and tongue (mostly) in cheek views. Get 30 days free. Political Fix was presented by Lucy Fisher and produced by Persis Love and Clare Williamson. Manuela Saragosa is the executive producer. Original music and sound engineering by Breen Turner. The global head of audio is Flo Phillips. Comments have not been enabled for this article.

articles.title: RH, the $3bn furniture brand, has moved into Mayfair
articles.raw_summary: As part of a global rollout, the US homeware behemoth now resides at a lavish London address
articles.extracted_text: Roula Khalaf, Editor of the FT, selects her favourite stories in this weekly newsletter. The 18th-century Palladian mansion at No 7 Burlington Gardens has been a noble home, a bank and an outlet of American clothing brand Abercrombie & Fitch. On 27 June, the property and two adjoining Grade II-listed buildings will open as “RH London, The Gallery in Mayfair”, the latest high-profile launch from the fast-expanding American home furnishings brand. “It’s the most exciting time of my life,” says 68-year-old chairman and CEO Gary Friedman from the RH headquarters in Corte Madera, California. Friedman has been building the RH brand since 2001, when he took over struggling home goods chain Restoration Hardware and reinvented it as a purveyor of upscale furniture. It now has nearly 100 stores across the US, Canada and Europe, some 7,300 employees and recently listed a net revenue for the fiscal year 2025 of $3.44bn (up 8.1 per cent on 2024). Its European expansion began in 2023 with RH England , a takeover of the Sir John Soane-designed stately home Aynho Park on the Northamptonshire-Oxfordshire border. Madrid, Brussels, Düsseldorf and Munich were followed by two grand statements: RH Paris, on the Champs-Élysées , and Milan, in a palazzo on Corso Venezia. The latter was celebrated by what Vogue called “Milan Design Week’s Glitziest Party”. Margot Robbie made an appearance; Bar Basso mixed the cocktails. The furniture skews big and beige. “People say, ‘Oh, Gary Friedman doesn’t like colour,’” acknowledges Friedman. Linen upholstery on the popular Cloud sofa runs from white and dove to charcoal and graphite. There’s lighting (from marble table lamps to tiered cast-glass chandeliers) and outdoor furniture (like the Brera armchairs, in teak and rope, £1,800), while new product line RH Estates includes the Diamond Table, with cast-stone pedestals topped in oak (from £5,310) – a 1990s design by Michael Taylor, creator of the “California Look”. Brera teak swivel lounge chair, from £2,520. BUY Glass Bubble chandelier by John Pomp, from £3,880. BUY The aesthetic is “very safe, but still enough of a design statement that people will ask where you got your sofa from”, says Simon Stewart, founder of London design gallery Charles Burnand, which is moving to Mayfair later this year. Meanwhile, designer Anouska Hempel, who has worked on the London store, sums up the RH universe as “global, genius, genuine”. Friedman is aware that opinion around RH is polarised: “We say, ‘Love us or leave us.’” Friedman grew up in Sonoma, California. “My father died when I was five years old; my mom had bipolar schizophrenia,” he recalls. “We lived in tiny little apartments.” By the age of 16 he was head of the household. He worked at The Gap, then at Williams Sonoma, including subsidiary brand Pottery Barn. His vision for RH is unique, he says: “No one’s ever built a home brand that’s remotely like this.” As well as the RH Galleries and RH Spaces (which consist of two private jets and a yacht), Friedman’s vision encompasses an interior design service. Internally, “we don’t use the word meetings, we say adventures”, says Friedman. “We say it’s not our company, it’s our cause.” Part of this “cause” is to inspire the best in people, he says, “and that’s way more important than selling furniture”. As in the US, many of the new European outposts combine retail and hospitality. In Mayfair, among the “champagne-lacquered Roman columns” and a central glass elevator designed by Foster

articles.title: Greetings to the participants, organisers and guests of the 15th International Ballet Competition
articles.raw_summary: Vladimir Putin sent greetings to the participants, organisers and guests of the 15 th International Ballet Competition, held in memory of Yury Grigorovich, an outstanding choreographer.
articles.extracted_text: All content on this site is licensed under Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International Vladimir Putin sent greetings to the participants, organisers and guests of the 15 th International Ballet Competition, held in memory of Yury Grigorovich, an outstanding choreographer. “Launched back in 1969, this competition has acquired a truly legendary status as one of the oldest, most important and spectacular highlights on the international cultural scene. Our country has always been famous for its rich choreographic traditions and has every right to be proud of them today. This is why talented dancers from many countries nurture ambitious and daring dreams of connecting with the great history of Russian ballet by performing at the Bolshoi Theatre. Of course, taking part in this prestigious competition is a great opportunity for young dancers to get into the spotlight and receive support from experienced teachers and mentors, while also discovering our capital and its landmarks. I am convinced that this anniversary competition will take place in an atmosphere of creativity and mutual understanding and will help us discover new talent as a celebration of beauty for all those who appreciate it.”

articles.title: Meeting with permanent members of the Security Council

Taking part in the meeting were Prime Minister Mikhail Mishustin Mishustin Mikhail Prime Minister of the Russian Federation , State Duma Speaker Vyacheslav Volodin Volodin Vyacheslav Chairman of the State Duma , Deputy Chairman of the Security Council Dmitry Medvedev Medvedev Dmitry Deputy Chairman of the Security Council of the Russian Federation , Chief of Staff of the Presidential Executive Office Anton Vaino Vaino Anton Chief of Staff of the Presidential Executive Office , Presidential Aide Nikolai Patrushev Patrushev Nikolai Aide to the President , Defence Minister Andrei Belousov Belousov Andrei Defence Minister of the Russian Federation , Interior Minister Vladimir Kolokoltsev Kolokoltsev Vladimir Interior Minister of the Russian Federation , Foreign Minister Sergei Lavrov Lavrov Sergei Minister of Foreign Affairs of the Russian Federation , Director of the Federal Security Service Alexander Bortnikov Bortnikov Alexander Director of the Federal Security Service , and Director of the Foreign Intelligence Service Sergei Naryshkin Naryshkin Sergei Director of the Foreign Intelligence Service . President of Russia Vladimir Putin: Colleagues, good afternoon. There are two items on the agenda today. The first pertains to the safeguarding of internal security and stability. The second concerns our relations – the development of our relations with our immediate neighbours. I propose that we begin with the domestic agenda. We have two speakers: Director of the Federal Security Service Alexander Bortnikov and Interior Minister Vladimir Kolokoltsev. Published in sections: News , Transcripts , Security Council

articles.title: Condolences to Acting President of Venezuela Delcy Rodriguez Gomez
articles.raw_summary: Vladimir Putin sent a message of condolences to Acting President of Venezuela Delcy Rodriguez Gomez in connection with the devastating earthquake that struck northwestern Venezuela.
articles.extracted_text: All content on this site is licensed under Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International Vladimir Putin sent a message of condolences to Acting President of Venezuela Delcy Rodriguez Gomez in connection with the devastating earthquake that struck northwestern Venezuela. “Please accept our deepest condolences on the tragic consequences of the devastating earthquake that struck northwestern Venezuela. Please convey my sincere condolences to the families and friends of the victims as well as wishes for a speedy recovery to all those affected by the natural disaster. We would like to express our solidarity and support to the friendly people of Venezuela at this difficult time.”

articles.title: Greetings to the 14th Engineers of the Future 2026 International Youth Industrial Forum
articles.raw_summary: Vladimir Putin sent his greetings to the participants in the 14 th Engineers of the Future 2026 International Youth Industrial Forum.
articles.extracted_text: All content on this site is licensed under Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International Vladimir Putin sent his greetings to the participants in the 14 th Engineers of the Future 2026 International Youth Industrial Forum. “Over the years, your forum has become a respected and highly popular platform, bringing together talented young professionals, scientists, educators, and students from technical universities and design bureaus, as well as representatives of defence and industrial enterprises. You can look forward to open and roundtable discussions, project presentations, and meetings with prominent politicians, public figures, and executives of major companies. And, of course, you will have the opportunity to exchange experiences, find like-minded peers, and discuss promising initiatives and practical ways to implement them. I would like to emphasise that today, amid the rapid development of advanced and innovative technologies, the training of highly qualified personnel, support for engineering creativity, and the creation of opportunities for the professional fulfilment of young people are of particular importance. I am confident that the forum will be substantive and productive, and that its participants will be able to bring their ideas and plans to life. Most importantly, you are sincerely striving to achieve success in your chosen fields and to contribute to your country through your creative work. Therefore, your aspirations and dreams will certainly come true.”
